release_goal: Ship PLDD portable enforcement kernel and consumer adapters, then dogfood-activate PLDD in agenticToolset itself with a proven rollback path. SCOPE EXPANDED 2026-07-20: dogfood activation moved in-scope for this release by explicit human decision (ctony) — supersedes prior fixture-only exit criteria.
release_exit_criteria: Every implementation file changed by Phase A and Phase B kernel/adapter logic is below src/*; only the dogfood-activation step itself may write live .agents state, hooks, router, and registries, and every such write is enumerated in a generated-file manifest with a proven rollback
Planted architecture and contract violations produce identical stable rule ids and exit codes through Codex and Claude Code entry paths
Versioned ownership relations preserve legacy exploration arrays and compile deterministic scenario skeletons with complete event coverage

Consumer adapters are proven against isolated fixtures first (manifest + rollback), THEN dogfood-activated live in agenticToolset; live activation gets its own G1 threat-model addendum covering blast radius on hooks/router/registries and a tested rollback/uninstall path
EPIC-PLDD-CONSUMER-ADAPTERS starts only after EPIC-PLDD-ENFORCEMENT-KERNEL completes

epics.title: PLDD portable enforcement kernel
epics.notes: Phase A only. All implementation changes below src/*. Zero new npm dependencies. Delivered fail-closed architecture/placement checking, stable diagnostics, contract-id parity, versioned ownership relations, scenario compilation, and fixture tests. Current dual-track configuration remains unchanged. G1+G2 signed off by ctony. Merged to master via PR #7 (42572ec). Real defect found and fixed during mutation-test hardening: compileGlob mid-path '**' never matched any file (commit 744a254).

epics.title: PLDD inactive consumer-project adapters
epics.notes: Phase B, both slices complete. Slice 1 (pl-consumer-adapter-parity): fixture-only Codex/Claude Code adapter conformance, merged PR #8 (ec87b4f). Slice 2 (pl-dogfood-activation): manifest-driven snapshot/atomic-apply/rollback mechanism, proven live against agenticToolset's own tree (activate .claude/commands/pl-check.md -> verify exact diff -> rollback -> verify empty diff), G1 (carried forward, covers both slices) + G2 both signed off by ctony, merged PR #9 (ccc08bc). Independent adversarial review found and fixed 2 real bugs in slice 1 and 3 real bugs (1 HIGH, 2 MEDIUM) in slice 2. First-ever PLDD write outside src/* in this repo; scope-guard extended with an explicit allowedPaths param (not bypassed) so every other caller's src/-only behavior is unchanged. Live activation was rolled back, not left permanently active -- host-adapters.md documents exactly what was proven vs. not (mechanism proven; no live-enforcement hook wired).

resume_log.note: Reflect phase completed; epic marked done. Only epic in current release (MVP) — release_exit_criteria still need a pass check before /ts-iteration:close.

All epics done (1/1). Release exit criteria 4/4 verified pass. Retro written to .agents/ts-project-planner/retrospectives/MVP-retro.md. plan.json refined_count incremented. GitHub milestone/Jira transition steps skipped — no MCP integration wired for this project.

1 epic queued: EPIC-DUAL-TRACK-ORCHESTRATION. No unresolved cross-release dependencies (depends_on=[]; MVP's only epic already done). GitHub milestone/Jira sprint steps skipped — no MCP integration wired for this project (same as MVP close).

All epics done (1/1). See Iter3-retro.md.

2 epics queued in topological order: EPIC-PLDD-ENFORCEMENT-KERNEL then EPIC-PLDD-CONSUMER-ADAPTERS. Phase B depends on Phase A. Both are src-only; dogfood activation excluded. GitHub milestone/Jira skipped because no MCP integration is wired.

type=epic. Router initialized at Think. Phase A src-only boundary active. Existing Spectra change pl-enforcement-kernel-phase-a detected with proposal done; continuation blocked until G1 human sign-off.

Test->Ship->Reflect run this session. G2 signed off by ctony (86.22% mutation score, 0 dependency vulns, 8/8 checklist). One real defect found and fixed during mutation-test hardening (compileGlob mid-path '**' never matched, commit 744a254). Merged to master via PR #7 (42572ec), conflicts against a stale .agents/ snapshot on master (edff5e7) resolved by taking the branch's live state (verified superset, no data loss). type=epic (no bugfix scope-escalation pre-check needed). depends_on EPIC-PLDD-ENFORCEMENT-KERNEL satisfied (done). /ts-deliver init called: project registry already initialized (2026-07-04, unchanged), so this ran as an epic-scoped reset rather than a full re-interview -- prior epic's final state.json archived to history.jsonl (epic_state_archived), state.json reset to phase=think for this epic, acpl_pattern_group='G2+G5' reused from idea-004.exploration_output (shared bounded contexts across Phase A/Phase B, same idea). branch_name not yet set -- pending Think framing.

Scope expanded 2026-07-20 (ctony): dogfood activation moved in-scope for this epic, split into two sequenced Spectra changes -- slice 1 (pl-consumer-adapter-parity, fixture-only Codex/Claude Code adapter conformance) and slice 2 (pl-dogfood-activation, not yet proposed). Slice 1: Think->Plan->Build->Review->Test->Ship run this session. G1 signed off by ctony (covers both slices). Review: independent adversarial subagent found and fixed 2 real security bugs (path traversal via descriptor entry, duplicate-host silent parity bypass). Test: initial Stryker run scored 47.23%, traced to genuine missing tests + a subprocess-only-reachable CLI block (not a tooling artifact as first suspected); fixed with real tests and a runCli() refactor for in-process testability; re-ran at 68.33% >= 60% threshold. G2 signed off by ctony. Merged to master via PR #8 (ec87b4f), branch codex/pldd-consumer-adapter-parity deleted. Epic NOT done -- slice 2 (dogfood activation, the higher-risk live-install half) still needs Think->Ship. Router state.json currently reflects slice 1's Ship as complete; next /ts-iteration:next (or direct router resume) should re-enter Think for slice 2 rather than Reflect, since the epic itself isn't finished.

Resumed via /ts-iteration:next -> /ts-deliver:init per tasks/TASK.md (active_epic already set to this epic and not done, so this continued the same epic's next slice rather than STOPping or activating a new one). Router state.json archived slice 1's final ship state to history.jsonl (event=slice_state_archived) and was reset to phase=think, slice='pl-dogfood-activation', branch_name=null pending Plan. Read all 5 required-reading docs from TASK.md: g1-threat-model.md (title already covers dogfood activation, Flow 2 STRIDE + blast radius + recovery path all written against live-install case), framing.md, never_automate.md, review/report.md, host-adapters.md, plus pl-adapter-conformance.mjs and pl-scope-guard.mjs (confirmed: flat src/-prefix filter, no allowlist mechanism exists today -- must be added in Plan). No new unknowns surfaced beyond the 2 items G1 already deferred to Plan (scope-guard allowlist mechanism, activation task-tracking shape) -- no fresh G1 needed, existing sign-off (ctony, 2026-07-20T02:35:00+08:00) carried forward as-is. Verified no pl-dogfood-activation Spectra change exists yet in openspec/changes/ (only pl-consumer-adapter-parity and archive present; no parked changes dir found) -- next step is /spectra-propose. Wrote slice2-think.md recording the resume decision and open items. Stray unrelated README.md edit still present in working tree per git status (same one noted at slice 1's Build phase) -- left untouched, will be excluded from any slice-2 commit same as before.

Router exited Think->Plan (phase_exit appended to history.jsonl). Ran /spectra-propose pl-dogfood-activation: created proposal.md, design.md, specs/pl-dogfood-activation/spec.md, tasks.md. design.md resolves both open decisions from G1/Think -- (1) extend pl-scope-guard.mjs's checkPhaseAScope with an optional allowedPaths parameter (default []) rather than bypassing the guard, preserving today's unconditional src/-only behavior for every other caller; (2) track activation as this full Spectra change with its own BDD scenarios rather than an ad-hoc Build checklist item, matching slice 1's rigor. spectra analyze found 9 Coverage + 2 Consistency findings on first pass, all false positives from formatting (backticked path mentions inside the Capabilities section prose were mis-parsed as extra capability names; requirement-name references in tasks.md were wrapped across markdown line breaks, breaking the cross-reference substring check) -- fixed both by de-backticking incidental path mentions in proposal.md's Capabilities bullets and rephrasing tasks.md task descriptions to keep each requirement name on one unbroken line; re-analyze came back Coverage/Consistency/Gaps clean, 3 Suggestion-severity findings (missing concrete examples) left unaddressed per the propose workflow's own rule to not block on Suggestions. spectra validate: pass. Change parked (spectra park pl-dogfood-activation) -- per the propose workflow's explicit contract, /spectra-apply was NOT invoked this session; router state.json artifacts.plan marked complete, current_phase remains 'plan' (Plan's own gate is G1, already carried forward and signed -- no G2 gate sits at Plan-exit, that's at Ship). Next session: /spectra-apply pl-dogfood-activation to auto-unpark and begin Build.

Ran /spectra-apply pl-dogfood-activation: unparked, all 9 tasks completed. Created branch codex/pldd-dogfood-activation off master before any implementation (never_automate.md item 5 forbids running dogfood install against master directly). Tasks 1.1/1.2: extended pl-scope-guard.mjs's checkPhaseAScope with an optional allowedPaths param (default []), zero-arg behavior regression-tested unchanged. Tasks 2.1/2.2: implemented src/scripts/pl-dogfood-activate.mjs (snapshot-before-write, then atomic tmp->rename apply, emits a manifest). Tasks 3.1/3.2: implemented src/scripts/pl-dogfood-rollback.mjs (restore from manifest, post-restore diff verification). Preflight caught a real artifact bug before any of this: proposal.md/design.md/tasks.md all referenced src/tests/pl-*.test.ts but the project's actual test convention is src/tests/unit/pl-*.test.ts -- fixed all three artifact files, spectra validate re-passed. Tasks 4.1/4.2 (the epic's first-ever live write outside src/*) paused for explicit human input via AskUserQuestion on what real content to activate, since this repo had zero existing PLDD-related .claude/hooks/ or .agents/ registry content to reuse as a target -- ctony chose 'adapter descriptors only' (install slice 1's Claude Code adapter file live, not a live-enforcement hook). Defined src/scripts/pl-dogfood-target-list.json (single target .claude/commands/pl-check.md; Codex adapter skipped since no .codex/ dir exists in this repo). Ran activation for real against agenticToolset's own tree: git status confirmed exactly that one path added, content byte-identical to the source template, and the harness live-picked-up the new pl-check slash command (visible in session tool listings) -- concrete proof the mechanism has real effect, not just a fixture simulation. Ran rollback: git status/diff confirmed empty afterward. Task 5.1: updated host-adapters.md's 'What's not proven' section to state precisely what this proved (the activate/rollback mechanism, using the adapter file as first payload) versus what it didn't (no live hook wired to run on every session; Codex adapter not activated) -- had to preserve an existing test's regex assertion (src/tests/unit/ts-pl-skill.test.ts) that checks for the phrase 'separate, explicitly-approved' when rewriting this section. Full suite: 364/364 pass, type-check clean throughout. Committed in two commits on the branch (3ea0b5e mechanism + design/spec/tasks; a second commit for the real-tree proof artifact + docs), both excluding the pre-existing stray README.md edit noted at every prior Build phase in this epic. Per design.md's explicit Migration Plan and scope boundaries, Build's job was to prove the mechanism safe via an activate-then-rollback round-trip, not to leave PLDD permanently activated in this repo -- the .claude/commands/pl-check.md file was rolled back and does not currently exist on disk; whether to leave a real activation live (not rolled back) is a Ship-time / G2 decision, not decided this session. Epic/slice NOT done -- Review->Test->Ship->Reflect remain for this slice.

Review->Test->Ship (partial) run this session for slice 2. Review: independent adversarial subagent found 6 findings -- 1 HIGH (partial apply-phase failure produced no manifest at all, since the manifest was only built via .map()'s single return value; a target 3-of-N throw would leave targets 1-2 live-modified with nothing to feed pl-dogfood-rollback.mjs -- fixed by building the manifest incrementally and attaching a partialManifest to any thrown error, with runCli writing it to --manifest-out before exiting non-zero), 2 MEDIUM (new_hash computed but never verified anywhere -- rollback now does a pre-restore integrity pass and aborts if a target diverged since activation, rather than silently overwriting an intervening edit; allowedPaths entries were unvalidated -- checkPhaseAScope now rejects '', '.', '..', '../'-prefixed entries), 3 LOW accepted with rationale (no symlink/realpath check, non-unique tmp filenames, TOCTOU -- all out of scope for design.md's single-operator/manual-invocation threat model). Re-verified the real-tree round-trip against agenticToolset's own tree with the fixed scripts: still exactly one path changed, still empty diff after rollback. Test: Stryker mutation run on pl-dogfood-activate.mjs/pl-dogfood-rollback.mjs/pl-scope-guard.mjs first scored 53.42% -- same class of gap as slice 1 (CLI/isMainModule/runCli only reachable via subprocess). Added in-process CLI tests (mirroring pl-scope-guard.test.ts's dynamic-import pattern) plus tests targeting each Review fix; re-ran at 88.03% >= 60% threshold (clears the 85% high bar). 394/394 tests pass, type-check clean, src-only scope guard clean, npm audit 0 vulns. Ship: ctony signed off G2 explicitly ('G2 signed off, proceed to Ship') -- recorded in state.gates.G2 following the same checklist shape as slice 1's G2 record. Asked via AskUserQuestion how far to take Ship (push+PR / push-only / stay-local); ctony chose push-only. Pushed branch codex/pldd-dogfood-activation to origin (5 commits: 3ea0b5e, 9c01e52, 9723e97, 2d33bad, df23d1b) -- no PR opened, ctony will open it themselves. Epic/slice NOT done -- not merged, Reflect not started. Next session or ctony: open the PR, merge, then run Reflect to close out this slice and (if this is the epic's last remaining slice) the epic itself.

PR #9 opened this session at ctony's explicit request; CI (test job) watched via gh pr checks --watch, passed; merged via gh pr merge --merge --delete-branch at ctony's explicit request ('merge the PR after CI passes'). Merge commit ccc08bc31ee24c0cda20d00b24aaa7903c8d66d3. Local master fast-forwarded, branch deleted locally + on origin. Reflect: ran /spectra-archive pl-dogfood-activation -- all artifacts/tasks complete, no warnings, 4 requirements synced into openspec/specs/pl-dogfood-activation/spec.md (new capability, no delta-merge conflicts since no prior main spec existed), archived to openspec/changes/archive/2026-07-20-pl-dogfood-activation/. Epic EPIC-PLDD-CONSUMER-ADAPTERS marked done (both slices shipped: slice 1 PR #8 ec87b4f, slice 2 PR #9 ccc08bc). active_epic reset to null. Both Iter4 epics (EPIC-PLDD-ENFORCEMENT-KERNEL, EPIC-PLDD-CONSUMER-ADAPTERS) are now done -- release_exit_criteria verification and /ts-iteration:close are the natural next step but were NOT run this session (bigger/more final action than what was asked; surface readiness to ctony rather than assume).

All epics done (2/2): EPIC-PLDD-ENFORCEMENT-KERNEL, EPIC-PLDD-CONSUMER-ADAPTERS. All 5 release_exit_criteria verified pass: (1) every implementation file below src/* except the reviewed dogfood-activation step itself, enumerated in a generated manifest with a proven rollback (verified twice, real-tree empty-diff round-trip) -- PASS; (2) planted architecture/contract violations produce identical stable rule ids and exit codes across Codex/Claude Code entry paths -- PASS (slice 1's 6-case fixture parity suite, 216/216 tests); (3) versioned ownership relations preserve legacy arrays and compile deterministic scenario skeletons with complete event coverage -- PASS (EPIC-PLDD-ENFORCEMENT-KERNEL, 86.22% mutation score); (4) consumer adapters proven on fixtures first then dogfood-activated live with a G1 addendum and tested rollback -- PASS (slice 1 fixtures, slice 2 live); (5) EPIC-PLDD-CONSUMER-ADAPTERS started only after EPIC-PLDD-ENFORCEMENT-KERNEL completed -- PASS (sequencing followed, verified via history.jsonl timestamps). No deferred epics. Retrospective written to .agents/ts-project-planner/retrospectives/Iter4-retro.md (release-level, references the existing EPIC-PLDD-ENFORCEMENT-KERNEL epic-level retro for Phase A detail). plan.json refined_count incremented 3->4. GitHub milestone/Jira transition steps skipped -- no MCP integration wired for this project (consistent with every prior iteration close). Discovery has been running in parallel per idea-004's notes -- run /ts-discover status to check for new ready items before starting Iter5.
